settings.gge9fAeKv0g_text: How do you know if you have oily skin?
settings.ggJXnLEnRqH_text: What to look for in a moisturiser for oily skin
settings.ggx-ifyHSbR_text: Moisturiser for oily faces: Ingredients to avoid
settings.ggGAJgV9tWf_text: Tips to stay glowing, not shiny
settings.ggsuc8uMOvV_text:
If you’ve got oily skin, you’re probably dealing with excess oil, clogged pores, and frequent breakouts. And you might be thinking, “Do I even need moisturiser? Won’t this make things worse?”

 

The answer is yes, you absolutely do need moisturiser. In fact, skipping it can cause dehydration, which signals your skin to produce even more oil to compensate.

 

The key isn’t avoiding hydration. It’s choosing lightweight, non-clogging formulas that deliver moisture without heaviness. That’s why, in this guide, we’ll look at how you can find the best moisturiser for oily skin. We’ll also include some of our favourite Korean products, all designed to give you a glow without ever leaving you shiny.
settings.ggETGZ8Sv5y_text: How do you know if you have oily skin?
settings.ggQU5aCn5I9_text:
Oily skin is more than just having a bit of extra shine in warm weather. You’ll notice:

 

  • Your T-zone becomes shiny within hours of cleansing
  • You struggle with clogged pores and congestion
  • Your skin is acne-prone/you frequently get breakouts
  • Your pores are more visible 
  • Your make-up separates on your face
  • Your skin remains shiny no matter which products you use

 

Oily skin can sometimes be mistaken for hydrated skin. However, it’s important to note that your skin can actually be oily and dehydrated at the same time, and this can worsen congestion and increase breakouts.
settings.ggbxIGQz7l3_text: What to look for in a moisturiser for oily skin
settings.ggw51K8muVj_text:
When you’re searching for the right moisturiser for oily skin, the goal isn’t to get rid of oil completely. It’s to balance it. The best moisturisers for oily skin hydrate without adding heaviness, help regulate sebum production, and support a clear, glass-skin complexion.

 

Here’s what to look out for:

  • Lightweight gels or gel-creams that absorb quickly and won’t leave a greasy residue
  • Non-comedogenic formulas to reduce the risk of clogged pores
  • Humectants, like glycerin or hyaluronic acid, to deliver hydration without excess oil
  • Oil-regulating ingredients, such as niacinamide, to help visibly refine pores and control shine
settings.ggh7fP9vFam_text: Moisturiser for oily faces: Ingredients to avoid
settings.ggeEQJIOEvb_text:
When your skin already produces excess oil, layering on the wrong moisturizer can quickly lead to blockages. Some ingredients may feel nourishing initially, but can worsen shine and breakouts over time.

 

Here’s what to avoid when picking a moisturiser:

 

  • Heavy butters like cocoa butter 
  • Petrolatum-heavy formulas 
  • Alcohol-heavy formulas that strip the skin too much and trigger rebound oil production
  • Thick cream formulations designed for very dry skin
  • Isopropyl myristate and isopropyl palmitate, which can clog pores 
  • Lanolin, which can feel too rich and trigger congestion
settings.ggGZrJGgPQ__text: Korean skincare offers some of the best moisturisers for oily skin, formulated with lightweight, fast-absorbing textures that prioritise hydration without heaviness. Here are some of our favourites.
settings.ggEb3LFnl-K_text: Designed specifically for breakout-prone skin, Pyunkang Yul is a lightweight cream that delivers hydration without clogging pores. It contains soothing ingredients like centella asiatica and mild exfoliating components to help calm inflammation while keeping congestion under control.
settings.ggr3oBTFvgv_text: 2. INNISFREE Cherry Blossom Glow Jelly Cream
settings.ggeAh5XSztk_text: With its bouncy gel consistency, INNISFREE Glow Jelly Cream delivers a burst of hydration without sitting heavily on the skin. It leaves a subtle luminous finish rather than a greasy sheen, which can work well for oily skin that leans dehydrated.
settings.ggraMpXZtby_text: 3. Beauty of Joseon Red Bean Water Gel
settings.ggRdar2RkDe_text: One of the most well-loved Korean moisturisers for oily skin, Beauty of Joseon Red Bean Water Gel is a water-based gel that hydrates without adding grease. Infused with red bean extract to help balance oil, it absorbs quickly and keeps skin feeling fresh, smooth, and shine-controlled throughout the day.
settings.ggO0mGMQrjT_text: If your skin is oily but easily irritated, IUNIK Centella Calming Gel Cream is the perfect solution for you. Its blend of centella asiatica, tea tree leaf water, and calendula extract delivers lightweight moisture while helping to calm irritation.
settings.ggsMkkqNclI_text: 5. COSRX Hydrium Green Tea Aqua Soothing Gel Cream
settings.ggycz9dDBu8_text: Formulated with green tea extract and hydrating ingredients like panthenol, COSRX Hydrium Green Tea Aqua Soothing Gel Cream helps soothe redness while delivering moisture that sinks in quickly. The texture feels fresh on application, making it especially suitable for humid climates or as a daytime moisturizer under SPF.
settings.gg98Bxkm-11_text: Tips to stay glowing, not shiny
settings.ggusix--Lyp_text:
Managing oily skin isn’t about fighting your natural oil production. It’s about working with it. The right moisturizer helps regulate sebum, maintain hydration, and support a strong skin barrier so your complexion looks balanced rather than slick.

 

  • To keep your glow intentional (not greasy), remember to:
  • Use thin, even layers instead of applying too much moisturiser at once
  • Avoid over-cleansing or harsh exfoliants which trigger rebound oil production
  • Build your entire skincare routine around your oily skin, not just your moisturiser choice

Oily skin only becomes difficult to manage when it’s treated aggressively or inconsistently. When you strip it, overload it, or constantly switch products, your barrier becomes compromised, and excess oil production follows. A lightweight, well-formulated moisturiser anchors your routine, helping your skin stay hydrated, resilient, and more predictable over time.
settings.ggIqWBTWyCh_text: Moisturiser doesn’t usually cause acne, but the wrong formula can. Heavy, pore-clogging ingredients or overly rich creams can trap oil and dirt, leading to congestion. Choosing a non-comedogenic (non-clogging) moisturiser that’s designed for oily or acne-prone skin significantly reduces the risk.
settings.ggqkr4J3Gfj_text: Breakouts typically happen when a product is too heavy and creates a thick layer that traps oil on the skin. If your moisturiser feels greasy, sits on the surface of the skin, or causes clogged pores, it’s too rich for you. The key is choosing lightweight, breathable formulas that hydrate without overwhelming.
settings.ggLMaq6qgBK_text: Do I need a moisturiser for oily skin?
settings.ggt_E3-LLwF_text: Yes! However, if you have oily skin, you should opt for lighter formulas. A gel moisturiser for oily skin is often the best choice because it delivers lightweight, water-based hydration without heaviness. Look for ingredients like glycerin, hyaluronic acid, niacinamide, and soothing extracts that help control oil.
